query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
28→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
29→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)
32→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
33→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
34→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
35→            }
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}
47→

1→/*
 2→ *  Copyright (c) 2023 David Allison <davidallisongithub@gmail.com>
 3→ *
 4→ *  This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under

12→ *
13→ *  You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with
14→ *  this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
15→ */
16→
17→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
18→
19→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD.IHTTPSession
20→import java.io.File
21→import java.io.InputStream
22→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
23→import java.nio.channels.Channels
24→
25→/**
26→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
27→ */
28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
29→    val contentLength: Long get() = end - start + 1
30→
31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→}
43→
44→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
45→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
46→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
47→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
48→        it.skip(header.start)
49→    }
50→}
51→

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.net.Uri
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.CookieManager
 23→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
 24→import android.webkit.WebResourceError
 25→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 26→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 27→import android.webkit.WebView
 28→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
 31→import androidx.annotation.LayoutRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat
 34→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsControllerCompat
 35→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 36→import androidx.lifecycle.flowWithLifecycle
 37→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.TtsVoicesDialogFragment
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.getViewerAssetLoader
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.localizedErrorMessage
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.pages.AnkiServer
 43→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 44→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 45→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}
213→

See the GNU General Public License for more details. 12→ * 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with 14→ * this program. If not, see . 15→ * 16→ * This file incorporates code under the following license 17→ * https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/2.1.34/pylib/anki/template.py 18→ * 19→ * Copyright: Ankitects Pty Ltd and contributors 20→ * License: GNU AGPL, version 3 or later; http://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl.html 21→ */ 22→ 23→package com.ichi2.libanki 24→ 25→import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound.VIDEO_EXTENSIONS 27→import com.ichi2.libanki.TemplateManager.PartiallyRenderedCard.Companion.avTagsToNative 28→import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.BackendUtils 29→import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.model.toBackendNote 30→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.NotInLibAnki 31→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.append 32→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.len 33→import com.ichi2.utils.deepClone 34→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendTemplateException 35→import org.intellij.lang.annotations.Language 36→import org.jetbrains.annotations.VisibleForTesting 37→import org.json.JSONObject 38→import org.jsoup.Jsoup 39→import org.jsoup.nodes.Document.OutputSettings 40→import java.io.File 41→import java.net.URI 42→import java.net.URISyntaxException 43→import java.nio.file.Paths 44→ 45→private typealias Union = Pair 46→private typealias TemplateReplacementList = MutableList> 47→ 48→/** 49→ * Template.py in python. Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 50→ * 51→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 52→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 53→ * 54→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 55→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 56→ * 57→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 58→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 59→ * the filter is skipped. 60→ */ 61→class TemplateManager { 62→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 63→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 64→ companion object { 65→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 66→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 67→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 68→ 69→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 70→ } 71→ 72→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 73→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 74→ for (node in nodes) { 75→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 76→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 77→ } else { 78→ results.append( 79→ Pair( 80→ null, 81→ TemplateReplacement( 82→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 83→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 84→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 85→ ) 86→ ) 87→ ) 88→ } 89→ } 90→ 91→ return results 92→ } 93→ 94→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 95→ val value = tag.valueCase 96→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 97→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 98→ } else { 99→ TTSTag( 100→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 101→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 102→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 103→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 104→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 105→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 106→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 107→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 108→ ) 109→ } 110→ } 111→ 112→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 113→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 114→ } 115→ } 116→ } 117→ 118→ /** 119→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 120→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 121→ * using the _private fields directly. 122→ */ 123→ class TemplateRenderContext( 124→ card: Card, 125→ note: Note, 126→ browser: Boolean = false, 127→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 128→ template: JSONObject? = null, 129→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 130→ ) { 131→ private var _card: Card = card 132→ private var _note: Note = note 133→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 134→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 135→ 136→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 137→ 138→ companion object { 139→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 140→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 141→ } 142→ 143→ fun fromCardLayout( 144→ note: Note, 145→ card: Card, 146→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 147→ template: JSONObject, 148→ fillEmpty: Boolean 149→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 150→ return TemplateRenderContext( 151→ card, 152→ note, 153→ notetype = notetype, 154→ template = template, 155→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 156→ ) 157→ } 158→ } 159→ 160→ /** 161→ * Returns the card being rendered. 162→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 163→ * infinite loop. 164→ */ 165→ fun card() = _card 166→ 167→ fun note() = _note 168→ fun noteType() = noteType 169→ 170→ @NeedsTest( 171→ "TTS tags `fieldText` is correctly extracted when sources are parsed to file scheme" 172→ ) 173→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 174→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 175→ try { 176→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 177→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 178→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 179→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 180→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 181→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 182→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 183→ ) 184→ } 185→ 186→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 187→ val qtext = parseVideos( 188→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 189→ mediaDir 190→ ) 191→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 192→ var qoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(qout.text, mediaDir) 193→ 194→ val atext = parseVideos( 195→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 196→ mediaDir 197→ ) 198→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 199→ var aoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(aout.text, mediaDir) 200→ 201→ if (!_browser) { 202→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 203→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 204→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 205→ } 206→ 207→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 208→ questionText = qoutText, 209→ answerText = aoutText, 210→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 211→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 212→ css = noteType().getString("css") 213→ ) 214→ } 215→ 216→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 217→ val proto = col.run { 218→ if (_template != null) { 219→ // card layout screen 220→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 221→ _note.toBackendNote(), 222→ _card.ord, 223→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 224→ fillEmpty, 225→ true 226→ ) 227→ } else { 228→ // existing card (eg study mode) 229→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 230→ } 231→ } 232→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 233→ } 234→ 235→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 236→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 237→ var questionText: String, 238→ var answerText: String, 239→ val questionAvTags: List, 240→ val answerAvTags: List, 241→ val css: String = "" 242→ ) { 243→ 244→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 245→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 246→ } 247→ 248→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 249→ fun applyCustomFilters( 250→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 251→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 252→ frontSide: String? 253→ ): String { 254→ // template already fully rendered? 255→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 256→ return rendered[0].first!! 257→ } 258→ 259→ var res = "" 260→ for (union in rendered) { 261→ if (union.first != null) { 262→ res += union.first!! 263→ } else { 264→ val node = union.second!! 265→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 266→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 267→ node.currentText = frontSide 268→ } 269→ 270→ var fieldText = node.currentText 271→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 272→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 273→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 274→ } 275→ } 276→ 277→ res += fieldText 278→ } 279→ } 280→ return res 281→ } 282→ } 283→ 284→ /** 285→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 286→ * 287→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 288→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 289→ */ 290→ abstract class FieldFilter { 291→ abstract fun apply( 292→ fieldText: String, 293→ fieldName: String, 294→ filterName: String, 295→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 296→ ): String 297→ } 298→ companion object { 299→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 300→ } 301→} 302→ 303→/** 304→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 305→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 306→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 307→ * we use the webview player, like AnkiMobile does 308→ * 309→ * `file:///` is used to enable seeking the video 310→ */ 311→@NotInLibAnki 312→@VisibleForTesting 313→fun parseVideos(text: String, mediaDir: String): String { 314→ return SOUND_RE.replace(text) { match -> 315→ val fileName = match.groupValues[1] 316→ val extension = fileName.substringAfterLast(".", "") 317→ if (extension in VIDEO_EXTENSIONS) { 318→ val path = Paths.get(mediaDir, fileName).toString() 319→ val uri = getFileUri(path) 320→ 321→ @Language("HTML") 322→ val result = 323→ """""" 324→ result 325→ } else { 326→ match.value 327→ } 328→ } 329→} 330→ 331→/** 332→ * Parses the sources of the ``, `

16→package com.ichi2.anki
17→
18→import android.content.Context
19→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
20→import androidx.webkit.WebViewAssetLoader
21→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
22→import timber.log.Timber
23→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
24→import java.io.File
25→import java.io.FileInputStream
26→
27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
28→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
29→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)
32→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
33→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
34→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
35→            }
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()
46→}
47→

17→package com.ichi2.anki.pages
18→
19→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD.IHTTPSession
20→import java.io.File
21→import java.io.InputStream
22→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
23→import java.nio.channels.Channels
24→
25→/**
26→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
27→ */
28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
29→    val contentLength: Long get() = end - start + 1
30→
31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→}
43→
44→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
45→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
46→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
47→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
48→        it.skip(header.start)
49→    }
50→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.previewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.Intent
 19→import android.net.Uri
 20→import android.os.Bundle
 21→import android.view.View
 22→import android.webkit.CookieManager
 23→import android.webkit.WebChromeClient
 24→import android.webkit.WebResourceError
 25→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 26→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 27→import android.webkit.WebView
 28→import android.webkit.WebViewClient
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.CallSuper
 31→import androidx.annotation.LayoutRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsCompat
 34→import androidx.core.view.WindowInsetsControllerCompat
 35→import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
 36→import androidx.lifecycle.flowWithLifecycle
 37→import androidx.lifecycle.lifecycleScope
 38→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 39→import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.TtsVoicesDialogFragment
 40→import com.ichi2.anki.getViewerAssetLoader
 41→import com.ichi2.anki.localizedErrorMessage
 42→import com.ichi2.anki.pages.AnkiServer
 43→import com.ichi2.anki.snackbar.showSnackbar
 44→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 45→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.launchIn
 46→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.onEach
 47→import timber.log.Timber
 48→
 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}
213→

Called TemplateManager for technical reasons (conflict with Kotlin typealias) 50→ * 51→ * This file contains the Kotlin portion of the template rendering code. 52→ * Templates can have filters applied to field replacements. 53→ * 54→ * The Rust template rendering code will apply any built in filters, and stop at the first 55→ * unrecognized filter. The remaining filters are returned to Kotlin, and applied using the hook system. 56→ * 57→ * For example, {{myfilter:hint:text:Field}} will apply the built in text and hint filters, 58→ * and then attempt to apply myfilter. If no add-ons have provided the filter, 59→ * the filter is skipped. 60→ */ 61→class TemplateManager { 62→ data class TemplateReplacement(val fieldName: String, var currentText: String, val filters: List) 63→ data class PartiallyRenderedCard(val qnodes: TemplateReplacementList, val anodes: TemplateReplacementList) { 64→ companion object { 65→ fun fromProto(out: anki.card_rendering.RenderCardResponse): PartiallyRenderedCard { 66→ val qnodes = nodesFromProto(out.questionNodesList) 67→ val anodes = nodesFromProto(out.answerNodesList) 68→ 69→ return PartiallyRenderedCard(qnodes, anodes) 70→ } 71→ 72→ fun nodesFromProto(nodes: List): TemplateReplacementList { 73→ val results: TemplateReplacementList = mutableListOf() 74→ for (node in nodes) { 75→ if (node.valueCase == anki.card_rendering.RenderedTemplateNode.ValueCase.TEXT) { 76→ results.append(Pair(node.text, null)) 77→ } else { 78→ results.append( 79→ Pair( 80→ null, 81→ TemplateReplacement( 82→ fieldName = node.replacement.fieldName, 83→ currentText = node.replacement.currentText, 84→ filters = node.replacement.filtersList 85→ ) 86→ ) 87→ ) 88→ } 89→ } 90→ 91→ return results 92→ } 93→ 94→ fun avTagToNative(tag: anki.card_rendering.AVTag): AvTag { 95→ val value = tag.valueCase 96→ return if (value == anki.card_rendering.AVTag.ValueCase.SOUND_OR_VIDEO) { 97→ SoundOrVideoTag(filename = tag.soundOrVideo) 98→ } else { 99→ TTSTag( 100→ fieldText = tag.tts.fieldText, 101→ lang = tag.tts.lang, 102→ voices = tag.tts.voicesList, 103→ otherArgs = tag.tts.otherArgsList, 104→ // The backend currently sends speed = 1, even when undefined. 105→ // We agreed that '1' should be classed as 'use system' and ignored 106→ // https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/issues/15598#issuecomment-1953653639 107→ speed = tag.tts.speed.let { if (it == 1f) null else it } 108→ ) 109→ } 110→ } 111→ 112→ fun avTagsToNative(tags: List): List { 113→ return tags.map { avTagToNative(it) }.toList() 114→ } 115→ } 116→ } 117→ 118→ /** 119→ * Holds information for the duration of one card render. 120→ * This may fetch information lazily in the future, so please avoid 121→ * using the _private fields directly. 122→ */ 123→ class TemplateRenderContext( 124→ card: Card, 125→ note: Note, 126→ browser: Boolean = false, 127→ notetype: NotetypeJson? = null, 128→ template: JSONObject? = null, 129→ private var fillEmpty: Boolean = false 130→ ) { 131→ private var _card: Card = card 132→ private var _note: Note = note 133→ private var _browser: Boolean = browser 134→ private var _template: JSONObject? = template 135→ 136→ private var noteType: NotetypeJson = notetype ?: note.notetype 137→ 138→ companion object { 139→ fun fromExistingCard(col: Collection, card: Card, browser: Boolean): TemplateRenderContext { 140→ return TemplateRenderContext(card, card.note(col), browser) 141→ } 142→ 143→ fun fromCardLayout( 144→ note: Note, 145→ card: Card, 146→ notetype: NotetypeJson, 147→ template: JSONObject, 148→ fillEmpty: Boolean 149→ ): TemplateRenderContext { 150→ return TemplateRenderContext( 151→ card, 152→ note, 153→ notetype = notetype, 154→ template = template, 155→ fillEmpty = fillEmpty 156→ ) 157→ } 158→ } 159→ 160→ /** 161→ * Returns the card being rendered. 162→ * Be careful not to call .q() or .a() on the card, or you'll create an 163→ * infinite loop. 164→ */ 165→ fun card() = _card 166→ 167→ fun note() = _note 168→ fun noteType() = noteType 169→ 170→ @NeedsTest( 171→ "TTS tags `fieldText` is correctly extracted when sources are parsed to file scheme" 172→ ) 173→ fun render(col: Collection): TemplateRenderOutput { 174→ val partial: PartiallyRenderedCard 175→ try { 176→ partial = partiallyRender(col) 177→ } catch (e: BackendTemplateException) { 178→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 179→ questionText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 180→ answerText = e.localizedMessage ?: e.toString(), 181→ questionAvTags = emptyList(), 182→ answerAvTags = emptyList() 183→ ) 184→ } 185→ 186→ val mediaDir = col.media.dir 187→ val qtext = parseVideos( 188→ applyCustomFilters(partial.qnodes, this, frontSide = null), 189→ mediaDir 190→ ) 191→ val qout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = qtext, questionSide = true) 192→ var qoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(qout.text, mediaDir) 193→ 194→ val atext = parseVideos( 195→ applyCustomFilters(partial.anodes, this, frontSide = qout.text), 196→ mediaDir 197→ ) 198→ val aout = col.backend.extractAvTags(text = atext, questionSide = false) 199→ var aoutText = parseSourcesToFileScheme(aout.text, mediaDir) 200→ 201→ if (!_browser) { 202→ val svg = noteType.optBoolean("latexsvg", false) 203→ qoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(qoutText, col, svg) 204→ aoutText = LaTeX.mungeQA(aoutText, col, svg) 205→ } 206→ 207→ return TemplateRenderOutput( 208→ questionText = qoutText, 209→ answerText = aoutText, 210→ questionAvTags = avTagsToNative(qout.avTagsList), 211→ answerAvTags = avTagsToNative(aout.avTagsList), 212→ css = noteType().getString("css") 213→ ) 214→ } 215→ 216→ fun partiallyRender(col: Collection): PartiallyRenderedCard { 217→ val proto = col.run { 218→ if (_template != null) { 219→ // card layout screen 220→ backend.renderUncommittedCardLegacy( 221→ _note.toBackendNote(), 222→ _card.ord, 223→ BackendUtils.to_json_bytes(_template!!.deepClone()), 224→ fillEmpty, 225→ true 226→ ) 227→ } else { 228→ // existing card (eg study mode) 229→ backend.renderExistingCard(_card.id, _browser, true) 230→ } 231→ } 232→ return PartiallyRenderedCard.fromProto(proto) 233→ } 234→ 235→ /** Stores the rendered templates and extracted AV tags. */ 236→ data class TemplateRenderOutput( 237→ var questionText: String, 238→ var answerText: String, 239→ val questionAvTags: List, 240→ val answerAvTags: List, 241→ val css: String = "" 242→ ) { 243→ 244→ fun questionAndStyle() = "$questionText" 245→ fun answerAndStyle() = "$answerText" 246→ } 247→ 248→ /** Complete rendering by applying any pending custom filters. */ 249→ fun applyCustomFilters( 250→ rendered: TemplateReplacementList, 251→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext, 252→ frontSide: String? 253→ ): String { 254→ // template already fully rendered? 255→ if (len(rendered) == 1 && rendered[0].first != null) { 256→ return rendered[0].first!! 257→ } 258→ 259→ var res = "" 260→ for (union in rendered) { 261→ if (union.first != null) { 262→ res += union.first!! 263→ } else { 264→ val node = union.second!! 265→ // do we need to inject in FrontSide? 266→ if (node.fieldName == "FrontSide" && frontSide != null) { 267→ node.currentText = frontSide 268→ } 269→ 270→ var fieldText = node.currentText 271→ for (filterName in node.filters) { 272→ fieldFilters[filterName]?.let { 273→ fieldText = it.apply(fieldText, node.fieldName, filterName, ctx) 274→ } 275→ } 276→ 277→ res += fieldText 278→ } 279→ } 280→ return res 281→ } 282→ } 283→ 284→ /** 285→ * Defines custom `{{filters:..}}` 286→ * 287→ * Custom filters can check `filterName` to decide whether it should modify 288→ * `fieldText` or not before returning it 289→ */ 290→ abstract class FieldFilter { 291→ abstract fun apply( 292→ fieldText: String, 293→ fieldName: String, 294→ filterName: String, 295→ ctx: TemplateRenderContext 296→ ): String 297→ } 298→ companion object { 299→ val fieldFilters: MutableMap = mutableMapOf() 300→ } 301→} 302→ 303→/** 304→ * The desktop version handles videos in an external player (mpv) 305→ * because of old webview codecs in python, and to allow extending the video player. 306→ * To simplify things and deliver a better result, 307→
